license: Public / open (NIST GIAB — no restrictions; no PHI). No credentials required.
notes: GIAB publishes no official checksums for the v4.2.1 truth artifacts (truth-vcf, truth-vcf-index, high-conf-bed), so md5/sha256 stay null and the fetch script logs a computed sha256 on first download (trust-on-first-use); asserting a single local download's hash as canonical would be weaker than that. For reference, a validated 2026-07-08 fetch (VCF verified well-formed via bcftools: 470 records / 70,996 reads in the example-panel slice) produced sha256 truth-vcf=adb4d4a50048aa13353a06b84fcfcbca09a5d17525efaa4cea44f8822e81175c, truth-vcf-index=23162712553413b10f88916c28bd1238deb703a7237be2910440773beea71894, high-conf-bed=fba9a57c36ec88e5d14ea3e259c8866c7935f4998d3ec0fa2d6c3962da5b5575 — pin these into the sha256 fields for enforced re-fetch if desired. The reads BAM source carries the REAL md5 GIAB publishes (HG002_corrected_md5sums.feb19upload.txt); only its small .bai index is downloaded (and md5-verified), because the whole 122G BAM is NEVER downloaded — --with-reads streams a region-restricted slice via `samtools view -M -L <panel.bed> -X` (validated end-to-end).

artifacts.notes: NIST v4.2.1 small-variant truth calls, chr1-22. The gold-standard 'answer key' for faithfulness checks.
Tabix index for the truth VCF; required for --panel-bed region restriction via tabix/bcftools.
High-confidence regions BED. Faithfulness claims are only valid inside these regions.

Illumina 2x250 novoalign reads. The whole BAM (122G) is NEVER downloaded. Only used as a remote source that --with-reads slices to a panel region via `samtools view -M -L <panel.bed>`, producing a small local subset BAM. The md5/index_md5 above are the REAL values GIAB publishes in HG002_corrected_md5sums.feb19upload.txt; index_md5 is enforced on the downloaded .bai.
